name: Manoj Shah
bio: Born 5 February 1955 in Mumbai, Manoj Sakarchand Shah began as a dance teacher before a play directed by Mahendra Joshi turned him toward theatre. Inspired by Badal Sircar, Utpal Dutt, and Vijaya Mehta, he made his directorial debut with Master Phoolmani at the Prithvi Theatre Festival in 1999 — a production that ran for sixteen years. Since then he has directed 96+ productions in Gujarati, Hindi, and English under his company Ideas Unlimited, working with 157+ freelance artists. His biographical plays — Mareez (200+ performances since 2004), Mohan No Masalo (starring Pratik Gandhi, Limca Book of Records), Hu Chandrakant Bakshi, Karl Marx In Kalbadevi — are credited with sparking a cultural renaissance in Gujarati theatre. He has collaborated with painters Bhupen Khakhar, Ghulam Mohammed Sheikh, and Atul Dodiya on set design, and worked with filmmakers Richard Attenborough, Ketan Mehta, Ashutosh Gowariker, and Anand Gandhi. His productions have toured the USA, UK, Canada, Dubai, Singapore, and Muscat, and been selected for the Prithvi Festival, NSD Festival, Kalaghoda, Mumbai Theatre Utsav, and the Theatre Olympics.
otherNotableWork: Cultural Consultant: Kai Po Che, Good Road

name: Atul Kumar
bio: Founder of The Company Theatre, Atul is a director and actor known for his innovative approach to staging. His guest collaborations with IU Productions have produced memorable work.

name: Sameera Iyengar
bio: A distinguished arts administrator and actor, Sameera has been instrumental in shaping the programming at some of Mumbai's leading cultural venues. Her performances with IU Productions bring a refined elegance to the stage.

name: Vivek Gomber
bio: An actor and producer known for his work in both commercial and independent cinema. Vivek's stage work with IU Productions showcases his range and commitment to meaningful storytelling.

name: Shyam Pathak
bio: A National School of Drama graduate and trained theatre actor, Shyam Pathak began his career on the Mumbai stage with Nadira Babbar's Ekjute Theatre Group before becoming widely known as Patrakaar Popatlal Pandey in Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah. His Gujarati roots and theatre training brought him into the Ideas Unlimited fold, where he performed in productions including Achalayatan. He has also appeared in Ang Lee's Lust, Caution alongside Anupam Kher.
otherNotableWork: TV: Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah (2008–present)
Film: Lust, Caution (2007, dir. Ang Lee)

name: Alpesh Dikshit
bio: A freelance actor based in Mumbai with an M.Com degree and formal theatre training, Alpesh Dikshit has worked across stage, television, and film. His theatre credits with Ideas Unlimited include Achalayatan and Amar Fal, both directed by Manoj Shah. On screen, he has appeared in Radha Krishna, Crime Patrol, and Adaalat, and in films including Udan Patolas.

name: Vimal Upadhaya
bio: A Gujarati theatre actor and set designer who has been a recurring collaborator with Ideas Unlimited Productions. His dual sensibility as performer and visual thinker is rare in Indian theatre. Credits with the company include Achalayatan (actor and set designer), Amar Fal, Jal Jal Mare Patang (as Balashankar Kantharia), and Mareez — working alongside names like Pratik Gandhi and Dharmendra Gohil.

name: Kahgesh Vaidya
bio: A Gujarati theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions, contributing to the company's work in bringing literary classics to the stage.

name: Shailendra Patel
bio: A theatre actor who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions across their Gujarati repertoire, bringing sincerity and commitment to every role.

name: Janam Shah
bio: A member of the Ideas Unlimited family both literally and artistically, Janam Shah has grown up around theatre and contributed as an actor in the company's productions. He has also worked as a cinematographer on short films including Dhong.

name: Alpesh Dhakan
bio: Born in Mumbai to a Gujarati family, Alpesh Dhakan made his acting debut in Ideas Unlimited's Achalayatan in 2008 after a chance encounter with Manoj Shah at Prithvi Theatre. His second play, Muktidhaam, saw him perform alongside Smriti Irani for over 80 shows. He went on to play Lord Ganesha in the hit television series Devon Ke Dev... Mahadev and Saurabh Bose in Kuch Rang Pyar Ke Aise Bhi. He has also appeared in the Gujarati film Kevi Rite Jaish and performed Gandhi Before Gandhi at the JAINA Convention in Antwerp.
otherNotableWork: TV: Kuch Rang Pyar Ke Aise Bhi (2016–2017)

name: Deval Trivedi
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions, bringing dedication and craft to the company's ensemble work.

name: Dhruv Barot
bio: A Gujarati theatre and film actor, Dhruv Barot has performed with Ideas Unlimited and appeared in the Gujarati play Kaka 24 Karat Na and the film Musafir Chhu Yaaro.

name: Paurav Shah
bio: A theatre and film professional who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions. He has also worked as an assistant director on films including Aankh Micholi and Armaand.

name: Ishan Doshi
bio: A playwright and actor who began his Ideas Unlimited journey as a cast member in Achalayatan. He later wrote Mohan No Masalo, a biographical one-man play about Mahatma Gandhi starring Pratik Gandhi and directed by Manoj Shah. The play was performed in Gujarati, Hindi, and English, and entered the Limca Book of Records for being staged in three languages in a single day.
otherNotableWork: Playwright: Mohan No Masalo (2015, starring Pratik Gandhi)

name: Priyesh Thaar
bio: A theatre actor based in Mumbai, Priyesh Thaar has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions in their Gujarati repertoire.

name: Pankaj Soni
bio: A Gujarati theatre and film actor who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions. He has appeared in the National Award-winning Gujarati film Hellaro as Devaji, and in Kamthaan.

name: Hussaini Dawavala
bio: A Mumbai-based theatre professional who has worked with Ideas Unlimited as assistant director and lighting designer on productions including Achalayatan and as an actor in Mareez. He has since established himself as an independent director with his company Theatrix, staging Hindi plays like The Three Musketeers and Traffic, and directing multiple short films. He also served as assistant director on the feature film Tum: A Dangerous Obsession.
otherNotableWork: Film: Tum: A Dangerous Obsession (2004, Asst. Director)

name: Akash Naik
bio: A choreographer and assistant director who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions, contributing movement design and directorial support to the company's productions.

name: Rajiv Bhatt
bio: A costume designer who has collaborated with Ideas Unlimited Productions, creating visual identities for the company's characters that serve the narrative and period of each production.

name: Suresh Joshi
bio: A Gujarati music composer and arranger trained in classical vocal under Guru Shri Batukbhai Bhatt up to Upantya Visharad. After moving to Mumbai in 1989 and a formative seven-year mentorship with composer Uday Mazumdar, he began composing independently for theatre and audio albums. His theatrical music credits with Ideas Unlimited include Achalayatan, Apurva Avsar (Transmedia award winner), Jal Jal Mare Patang, Saraswati Chandra, Kalapi, and Mrityunjay, among others. He has also released numerous independent audio albums and lectured on music at Bhavan's Cultural Centre.
otherNotableWork: Album: Darr - Udi Jaay (Gujarat Govt. initiative)

name: Devki
bio: Devaki Marks, known as RJ Devaki, is an actor, radio jockey, and speaker from Ahmedabad, hailed as 'The Golden Voice of Ahmedabad' by the Governor of Gujarat. Born into Gujarati theatre's first family, she has performed in over 30 full-length plays including Undhioo, Manvi ni Bhavai, and Devdas, staged internationally and at the Bharat Rang Mahotsav. As Regional Programming Head at Red FM Gujarat, she won the Best Interview Show in the World at the New York Festival. For Ideas Unlimited, she performs the solo Gujarati play Adbhut — an interactive exploration of mental health adapted from Duncan Macmillan's Every Brilliant Thing.
otherNotableWork: Award: Best RJ Gujarat (India Radio Forum)

name: Satchit Puranik
bio: A reclusive writer, theatre maker, and filmmaker, Satchit Puranik graduated from FTII Pune in TV Direction. He has staged over 1,000 shows across a dozen countries in nine languages. For Ideas Unlimited, he wrote Adbhut and Tabiyat, and has performed Karl Marx In Kalbadevi for over a decade. In film, he edited Anand Gandhi's Ship of Theseus and handled casting for Chaitanya Tamhane's Court — two of the most acclaimed Indian independent films of the 2010s.

name: Amit Bhavsar
bio: A Gujarati music director and composer, and father of singer-composer Siddharth Amit Bhavsar. He composed the music for Ideas Unlimited's Adbhut.

name: Siddharth Amit Bhavsar
bio: A singer, composer, and lyricist from Ahmedabad known online as Musicwaala. Son of music director Amit Bhavsar. His breakout was the song Dhun Laagi from Love Ni Bhavai, and he has since composed for Gujarati films including Sharato Lagu, Short Circuit, and Chabutro, as well as Hindi singles. He contributed songs to Ideas Unlimited's Adbhut.
otherNotableWork: Award: Playback Singer of the Year (Gujarati Iconic Film Awards, 2019)

name: Madhubanti Bagchi
bio: A playback singer and composer from Balurghat, West Bengal, trained in Agra Gharana classical vocals under Shrimati Subhra Guha. She has recorded for Pritam, Amit Trivedi, Sachin-Jigar, and Sanjay Leela Bhansali. Her song Aaj Ki Raat from Stree 2 crossed 765 million YouTube views. She also holds degrees in Electronics Engineering and Laser Engineering. She contributed vocals to Ideas Unlimited's Adbhut.
otherNotableWork: Composers: Pritam, Amit Trivedi, Sachin-Jigar, Sanjay Leela Bhansali

name: Yashika Sikka
bio: A playback singer and voice-over artist whose Bollywood debut was Sayani from Padman. She has since sung for Manmarziyaan and worked with A.R. Rahman and Imtiaz Ali on Amar Singh Chamkila, singing Tu Kya Jaane. A trained classical singer, she contributed vocals to Ideas Unlimited's Adbhut.
otherNotableWork: Song: Tu Kya Jaane (Amar Singh Chamkila, 2024, with A.R. Rahman)

name: Nizamuddin Shah
bio: An actor, writer, and theatre practitioner based in Mumbai. He appeared in Sooni Taraporevala's Netflix film Yeh Ballet and is the older brother of Amiruddin Shah, the real-life ballet dancer whose story inspired the film. He runs a street theatre group in Mumbai with performers from low-income families, staging awareness plays on public health and social issues. He serves as stage manager for Ideas Unlimited's Adbhut.

name: Sandeep Jaiswar
bio: A stage manager who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions.

name: Priyank Patel
bio: A music operator who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions, managing live sound for their performances.

name: Dayashankar Pandey
bio: Born in Mumbai to an Awadhi-speaking family from Bhadohi, Uttar Pradesh, Dayashankar Pandey began his film career with Pehla Nasha in 1993. He went on to appear in Lagaan, Gangaajal, Swades, and Raajneeti before becoming widely known as Inspector Chalu Pandey in Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah, where he also serves as creative consultant. For Ideas Unlimited, he took on the title role in Bhamasha, spending weeks working on the Rajasthani dialect to inhabit the 16th-century Jain warrior.
otherNotableWork: TV: Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah (2010–present)

name: Dharmendra Gohil
bio: A film actor and voice artist who started in Gujarati theatre before moving to Marathi and Hindi cinema. A regular Ideas Unlimited collaborator, he has appeared in Bhamasha, Mareez, and Amar Fal. His film credits include Gandhi, My Father, 102 Not Out, Kasoombo, and Baby John.

name: Om Puri
bio: One of India's most celebrated actors, Om Puri's career spanned Bollywood, Hollywood, and British cinema with over 300 films. He lent his voice as narrator for Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha, bringing gravitas to the historical epic through recorded narration.

name: Yashpal Sharma
bio: Born in Hisar, Haryana, Yashpal Sharma developed his love for acting through local Ramlila performances before graduating from the National School of Drama in 1994. After starting with the NSD Repertory Company, he moved to Mumbai and built a formidable film career spanning Lagaan, Gangaajal, Ab Tak Chhappan, Hazaaron Khwaishein Aisi, and Singh Is Kinng. He contributed voice-over work to Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha.

name: Chugge Khan
bio: A Manganiyar maestro from the Jaisalmer-Barmer region of Rajasthan, Chugge Khan began performing at age four and toured internationally by thirteen. He founded Rajasthan Josh, a collective of Manganiyar musicians, in 2009. A master of Morchang, Khartal, and Bhappang, his repertoire spans Sufi qawwali, traditional Rajasthani folk, and the poetry of Kabir and Bulleh Shah. He led the live folk orchestra for Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha. His music has been featured on Coke Studio and in the film Junun.
otherNotableWork: Albums: Rajasthan Josh, Baraat, Desert Lounge

name: Kabir Thakore
bio: An Ahmedabad-based architect and theatre veteran who serves as convener of Rangmandal, a 75-year-old theatre institution. He built Scrapyard Theatre in the courtyard of his residence — a space for artists to learn and perform without financial barriers. Active in theatre mentorship since 1999, he designed the sets for Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha, evoking Mughal-era palaces and Rajput fortresses on stage.

name: Dr. Bipin Doshi
bio: The author of the original story for Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha, Dr. Bipin Doshi brought the overlooked history of the Jain warrior-philanthropist to the stage.

name: Mihir Bhuta
bio: A dramatist who has written and adapted scripts for Ideas Unlimited Productions, including Jal Jal Mare Patang (the biographical play about philosopher Manilal Dwivedi, 2009), Bhamasha (the Hindi historical drama, 2013), and the Hindi script for Mohan Ka Masala.

name: Kanhaiya
bio: A music composer who created the original score for Ideas Unlimited's Bhamasha, blending dramatic theatrical music with Rajasthani folk traditions.

name: Ahlam Khan
bio: Daughter of the legendary actor Amjad Khan — Gabbar Singh of Sholay — Ahlam Khan Karachiwala holds an MA in English Literature from Mumbai University and has built a distinguished theatre career in her own right. She won Best Actress at the Mahindra Excellence in Theatre Awards (META) for Aaj Rang Hai. For Ideas Unlimited, she has appeared in Bhamasha and Amar Fal. She works across English, Gujarati, and Hindi theatre.
otherNotableWork: Award: Best Actress, META Awards (Aaj Rang Hai)

name: Jay Upadhyay
bio: A Mumbai-based actor active in professional theatre since 2001, Jay Upadhyay is best known for playing Pranav Seth in Scam 1992: The Harshad Mehta Story. He starred in the Gujarati film Kevi Rite Jaish, credited with starting the era of urban Gujarati cinema, and won the Transmedia Award for Best Actor in Comic Role for Paaghdi. A core member of Manoj Shah's theatre group, he has appeared in Bhamasha and Mareez.
otherNotableWork: Award: Best Actor Comic Role (Transmedia, Paaghdi)

name: Pulkit Solanki
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions.

name: Aishwarya Mehta
bio: A theatre actor who has appeared in Ideas Unlimited productions including Bhamasha and Mareez.

name: Sagar Rawal
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions across multiple Gujarati and Hindi productions including Bhamasha and Amar Fal.

name: Dr. Jitendra B. Shah
bio: A leading Jain scholar and Director of the L.D. Institute of Indology in Ahmedabad (1998–2019), Dr. Jitendra B. Shah is well-versed in Sanskrit, Pali, and Prakrit. He has authored 12 books and over 150 research articles, added 10,000 manuscripts to the Institute's collection, and coordinated the Dalai Lama's meeting with Jain Acharyas on Non-violence and Religion in 2010. He distilled the 16,000-verse Upamitibhavaprapancha Katha by Siddharshi Gani into the theatrical concept for Ideas Unlimited's Bhav Prapanch. He has been honoured with the Jain Padmashree and Nagar-Ratna of Ahmedabad.

name: Prathang Dave
bio: A writer who co-wrote the script for Ideas Unlimited's Bhav Prapanch, adapting the ancient Jain allegorical text by Siddharshi Gani for the stage.

name: Raju Dave
bio: A writer who has collaborated with Ideas Unlimited Productions, co-writing the scripts for Bhav Prapanch and Apurva Avsar with Manoj Shah.

name: Hemang Vyas
bio: A National School of Drama alumnus with a postgraduate degree from Mumbai University, Hemang Vyas has over sixteen years of theatre experience and founded the company Claptrap. His film credits include Sanju and Bawaal. He performed in Ideas Unlimited's Bhav Prapanch.

name: Kashyap Pathak
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions.

name: Jitu Mehta
bio: A theatre and film actor who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions. His film credits include Gujarat 11 and Corporate.

name: Bhotesh Vyas
bio: A lighting designer who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions, creating the visual atmosphere for their stage productions.

name: Bhamini Oza Gandhi
bio: A celebrated Gujarati theatre and screen actress known for playing Kissmi and Rita in the iconic sitcom Sarabhai vs Sarabhai. Wife of actor Pratik Gandhi and a cancer survivor, she has appeared in Kathal: A Jackfruit Mystery, Aum Mangalam Singlem, and Bhavai. She has been cast as Kasturba Gandhi in Hansal Mehta's upcoming series Gandhi. For Ideas Unlimited, she played Ruttie Petit in Bombay Flower — a performance the Parsiana magazine described as capturing both the exuberance and the disintegration.

name: Vishal Shah
bio: A young actor from Ahmedabad who dropped CA studies to pursue theatre full-time. He founded Indie Productions, one of Gujarat's most recognised theatre companies, and won the Transmedia Award for Best Actor (Stage) in his debut year. He has acted in 18 theatre productions with 12 lead roles. For Ideas Unlimited, he played Muhammad Ali Jinnah in Bombay Flower.

name: Geeta Manek
bio: A Gujarati author, playwright, and journalist who spent fifteen years as assistant editor at Femina Gujarati before turning to playwriting full-time. Her plays for Ideas Unlimited include Bombay Flower and Dr Anandibai Joshi: Like, Comment, Share — the latter performed in three languages and invited to international theatre festivals. She also wrote Akhir Kyon, described as the first play worldwide about 9/11, and Sagpan Na Saudagar, which ran for over 100 shows. Her docu-novel Sardar: The Game Changer was serialised in Midday Gujarati.

name: Naman Sheth
bio: A theatre actor who played Kanji Dwarkadas — friend of the Jinnahs — in Ideas Unlimited's Bombay Flower.

name: Rishabh Kamdar
bio: A theatre actor who played Sir Dinshaw Petit in Ideas Unlimited's Bombay Flower, portraying the father whose daughter's elopement with Jinnah fractured one of Bombay's most prominent Parsi families.

name: Purvi Desai
bio: A theatre and film actress with over twelve years of stage experience before her Gujarati film debut in Halkie Fulkee. For Ideas Unlimited, she played the dual roles of Lady Dinbai Petit and Fatima Jinnah in Bombay Flower — the mother who lost her daughter and the sister who gained one.

name: Dhruv Dave
bio: A versatile actor with experience across television, film, and theatre, Dhruv Dave has built a diverse portfolio in both Hindi and Gujarati entertainment. His television credits include Sapno Ki Chhalang and Crime Patrol on Sony TV, and Swaraj on DD National. With Ideas Unlimited, he has performed in Bombay Flower and 3rd Bell. Deeply rooted in Gujarati and Hindi stage productions, his theatre experience lends nuance and emotional depth to his screen work. He also brings a keen understanding of visual storytelling through his background in editing.

name: Pinkesh Prajapati
bio: A Mumbai-based actor and theatre professional with nine years of stage experience, Pinkesh Prajapati has been part of Ideas Unlimited productions including Socrates, iSchool, Tabiyat, Lagan Magan, and Mr. Apple, while also serving as assistant to director Manoj Shah across multiple productions. Trained in Kalaripayattu and authentic Garba folk dance, he brings a physical, grounded presence to his work across stage and screen.

name: Manasi Joshi
bio: A Marathi and Gujarati theatre actress, Hindustani classical singer, and voice artist with over thirteen years of stage experience. She has performed over 550 shows of the Marathi musical Sangeet Devbabhali, appeared in Anthony Maras's international film Hotel Mumbai alongside Dev Patel and Anupam Kher, and played Yamini in Sony SAB's Wagle Ki Duniya for 647 episodes. As a voice artist for Disney India, she has dubbed Phineas in Phineas & Ferb and roles in The Lion King and Oggy and the Cockroaches. For Ideas Unlimited, she has performed the solo role in Dr. Anandibai Joshi: Like, Comment, Share since 2017 — a 75-minute monologue she describes as an actor's dream role. She has won META, Zee Marathi, and Maharashtra State Government awards.
otherNotableWork: Film: Hotel Mumbai (2018, with Dev Patel)

name: Hemant Joshi
bio: A lighting designer who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions, creating the visual atmosphere for Dr. Anandibai Joshi: Like, Comment, Share.

name: Jatin Vaja
bio: A music operator who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions.

name: Pratik Gandhi
bio: Born in Surat on 28 April 1980 to a family of teachers, Pratik Gandhi trained as an industrial engineer at North Maharashtra University, Jalgaon (2004), and spent a decade holding day jobs — first at the National Productivity Council in Satara and Pune (2004–07), then at Reliance Infrastructure's cement division in Mumbai (January 2008–2016) — while building a parallel life in Gujarati theatre. He debuted on stage in 2005 with Aa Paar Ke Pele Paar and took on dense character work in Apurva Avsar (2007, six roles), Saat Tari Akvees (2008–09), Bohot Nachyo Gopal (2012, as Krishna), Ame Badha Sathe To Duniya Laiye Mathe (2013, seven characters) and Sir Sir Sarla (2018); his earliest screen credits were Sridhar Rangayan's English indie Yours Emotionally (2006) and 68 Pages (2007). Manoj Shah spotted him on tour and cast him in two solo plays that defined his stage craft — Hu Chandrakant Bakshi (2013), inhabiting the controversial Gujarati writer, and Mohan No Masalo (2015), where he played the young Mohandas Gandhi in Gujarati, Hindi and English on the same day, earning a Limca Book of Records entry. In Gujarati cinema he broke through as Tapan 'Tino' in Abhishek Jain's Bey Yaar (2014), led the National Award–winning Wrong Side Raju (2016), and anchored Tamburo and Love Ni Bhavai (2017), Ventilator (2018), Dhunki and Gujarat 11 (2019), and Luv Ni Love Storys (2020), with Hindi crossovers in Loveyatri and Mitron (2018). Scam 1992: The Harshad Mehta Story (SonyLIV, 2020) — Hansal Mehta's biographical drama in which he played stockbroker Harshad Mehta — made him a national sensation; his follow-ups include Bhavai (2021, originally Raavan Leela), Atithi Bhooto Bhava, Kahani Rubberband Ki and Vaahlam Jaao Ne (2022), the OHO Gujarati web series Vitthal Teedi (2021), Modern Love Mumbai's 'Baai' episode and The Great Indian Murder (2022), a Scoop cameo (2023), the Spotify crime podcast Gangistan (2021–22, 48 episodes), and a 2024 run of Madgaon Express, Do Aur Do Pyaar, Dedh Bigha Zameen and Agni. In 2025 he led Dhoom Dhaam, the Jyotirao Phule biopic Phule, and Netflix's espionage drama Saare Jahan Se Accha: The Silent Guardians; on the slate are the Taapsee Pannu co-starrer Woh Ladki Hai Kahaan?, the Saif Ali Khan–led Netflix election drama Hum Hindustani, and a return to Mahatma Gandhi as lead in Hansal Mehta's multi-season Gandhi series for Applause Entertainment. Gandhi still calls Manoj Shah between film projects to perform shows.

otherNotableWork.title: Gandhi
otherNotableWork.role: Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi (Lead) · dir. Hansal Mehta

otherNotableWork.title: Hum Hindustani
otherNotableWork.role: Netflix film · with Saif Ali Khan — India's first election drama

otherNotableWork.title: Saare Jahan Se Accha: The Silent Guardians
Agni
otherNotableWork.role: Vitthalrao Dhonduba Surve · dir. Rahul Dholakia

otherNotableWork.title: Scoop
otherNotableWork.role: Man on Road (uncredited cameo, Ep. 6) · Netflix

otherNotableWork.title: Kehvatlal Parivar
otherNotableWork.role: Special appearance in song 'Holi Aavi Aavi' · Gujarati

otherNotableWork.title: Bhavai
otherNotableWork.role: Rajaram Joshi (Lead) · originally titled Raavan Leela

otherNotableWork.title: Wrong Side Raju
otherNotableWork.role: Raju Bambani (Lead) · National Award, Best Gujarati Film

otherNotableWork.title: Ame Badha Sathe To Duniya Laiye Mathe
otherNotableWork.role: 7 characters (Popat, Akhil, Vimal, Kaka, Naresh, etc.) · Gujarati

interviews.title: Pratik Gandhi on his theatre life with Manoj Shah
Manoj Shah's new play discovers the early days of Gandhi the legend
Pratik Gandhi Interview: I Work on My Instincts
Theatre is what keeps him grounded like a root

name: Shishir Ramavat
bio: A Gujarati journalist, novelist, and playwright from Jamnagar. He trained in computer engineering at M.S. University, Baroda before leaving to pursue journalism — working at Janmabhoomi Pravasi, Chitralekha, Mid-Day (as Features Editor), and Abhiyaan (as Executive Editor). He currently serves as News/Magazine Editor at Gujarat Samachar. He wrote Hu Chandrakant Bakshi for Ideas Unlimited, drawing on Bakshi's autobiography Bakshinama. He has authored eight books including four novels and served as Gujarati Language Expert at Microsoft's Hyderabad headquarters.

name: Vedish Jhaveri
bio: A Gujarati theatre actor who played the lead role of Manilal Dwivedi in Ideas Unlimited's Jal Jal Mare Patang — a demanding performance depicting the philosopher's life from youth to death at forty.

name: Kalpana Shah
bio: A theatre actress who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions, playing Manilal Dwivedi's mother in Jal Jal Mare Patang and appearing in Amar Fal.

name: Unnati Gala
bio: A freelance actress who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions in Kaagdo, Third Bell, and Tabiyat. On screen, she has appeared in A Thursday alongside Yami Gautam, Amazon's Paatal Lok, and Mumbaikar. She plays the daughter opposite Jay Upadhyay in Kaagdo.

name: Uttam Gada
bio: The writer of Karl Marx In Kalbadevi — Ideas Unlimited's most performed and most provocative play. Gada's script imagines what would happen if Karl Marx landed in Kalbadevi, Mumbai's most unapologetically capitalist neighbourhood. The play has run for over twelve years, been performed in Hindi and Hinglish, and staged at venues from Prithvi Theatre to Wilson College to international conventions.

name: Vinit Shukla
bio: The playwright who adapted Raeesh Maniar's biography of poet Mareez into the stage play that became Ideas Unlimited's longest-running and most performed production. His script weaves poetry, hallucination, and realism into a non-linear narrative that has been performed over 200 times since 2004.

name: Kumkum Das
bio: A Gujarati theatre and film actress who arrived in Mumbai from Baroda with a Science degree from M.S. University. She began as a school teacher before entering theatre. Under Manoj Shah's direction, she plays Sona — Mareez's wife — offering a grounded counterpoint to the poet's drifting spirit, embodying resilience and silent suffering across 200+ performances.

name: Uday Mazumdar
bio: A tabla player, music producer, and composer trained under Ravi Shankar in the guru-shishya parampara. He has accompanied Ravi Shankar and Zakir Hussain, performed for the British and Swedish royal families, and released over twelve albums. He composed the soulful music for Ideas Unlimited's Mareez, creating the sonic landscape for the poet's journey. He was also the mentor of Suresh Joshi, Ideas Unlimited's regular music director.

name: Darshan Pandya
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions in Mareez and other productions.

name: Pradeep Vengurlekar
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions in Mareez.

name: Satya Mehta
bio: The writer of the original Gujarati script for Mohan No Masalo, the biographical solo play about young Mohandas Gandhi that entered the Limca Book of Records.

name: Atul Dodiya
bio: One of India's foremost contemporary artists, deeply touched by Mahatma Gandhi's teachings. He created the black-and-white backdrop paintings for Ideas Unlimited's Mohan No Masalo — images of a young Gandhi that served as the visual world for Pratik Gandhi's solo performance. His earlier collaboration with Ideas Unlimited on Jal Jal Mare Patang (2009) established the painter-theatre partnership that defines Shah's visual language. His work has been exhibited at the Venice Biennale and is held in major collections worldwide.

name: Parthiv Gohil
bio: A versatile Gujarati playback singer known for songs like Yoon Shabnami, Punjabi Munde, and Daras Bina Nahin Chain. From soulful ghazals to super-energised folk numbers, he contributed the vocal performances for Ideas Unlimited's Mohan No Masalo.

name: Arpit Jain
bio: Co-writer of the Hindi script for Mohan Ka Masala, the Hindi-language version of Ideas Unlimited's trilingual Gandhi play.

name: Chirag Vohra
bio: Mumbai-born actor who debuted professionally in 1999 as the original lead of Master Phoolmani — Ideas Unlimited Productions' very first play — where he played the triple role of Manilal, Phoolmani and Vanlata across more than a hundred performances over sixteen years. For that early female-lead he would call women friends home and have them read his scenes aloud, listening for the inner sur of a voice he had not yet lived inside; the instinct has stayed with him. 'The preparation method is the same,' he has said of moving between stage and camera, 'only the execution changes.' A foundational member of Manoj Shah's ensemble, he has performed in Akho Akha Bolo, Huto and Huto Oh Yaa!, Gujarati Ni Asmita, Lakshmi Poojan, Mareez and the solo comedy What's Up? (NCPA CentreStage Festival, 2015); beyond Ideas Unlimited he has played Mahatma Gandhi on stage in Bharat Bhagya Vidhata and appeared in Umesh Shukla's Kishan vs Kanhaiya and Gandhi Ke Godse. On screen he is widely recognised as Bhushan Bhatt in Hansal Mehta's Scam 1992: The Harshad Mehta Story, and as Mahatma Gandhi in Nikkhil Advani's Freedom at Midnight (SonyLIV, 2024) — a role for which he reportedly lost fifteen kilos. Other OTT work includes Scoop, Crash Course and Lootere; his television credits include Hum Paanch, Sumit Sambhal Lega and OMG: Oh My God! His films span Mangal Pandey: The Rising, Lage Raho Munna Bhai, Heyy Babyy, Kidnap, Billu, Tere Bin Laden and its sequel Dead or Alive, Commando 2, Toba Tek Singh, Yamla Pagla Deewana Phir Se, Rashmi Rocket, the short Ek Raat and the Gujarati feature Locha Laapsi — with early uncredited turns in Kucch To Hai and Jaago. Chirag treats fame as a by-product of the work: you should be able to look at yourself in the mirror, he believes, and know honestly how much of yourself you gave a performance. Even now, a slight anxiety before a first show, and rehearsal after rehearsal until the anxiety settles, is how a role is earned — one sur, one polish at a time.

name: Hemant Kher
bio: A theatre actor who has performed with Ideas Unlimited Productions in Third Bell.

name: Disha Savla Upadhyay
bio: A seasoned actor with over three decades of experience across theatre and screen, Disha Savla Upadhyay is known for her ease across languages, forms, and characters. With more than 60 plays in Gujarati, Hindi, and English — spanning both commercial and experimental spaces — her theatre work includes Saraswatichandra, Mann Male Tyan Melo, Aa Family Fantastic Che, The Play That Goes Wrong, Raman Bhaman, Mr. Apple, Third Bell, and the solo performance Clean Bold. On screen, she became widely known as Bobby Chaubey in Zee TV's Neeli Chatri Waale (nominated for Best Actress in a Comic Role), and has since appeared in Narsinh Mehta (Best Actress), Madam Sir, Meri Bhavya Life, and over 20 episodes of Crime Patrol. Her film credits include Hasee Toh Phasee and Gujarati films such as Char Fera Nu Chakdol, Halkee Fulkee, and Swagatam. She will next be seen in Hansal Mehta's Gandhi as Putalibai, and in the Gujarati film Lakhpat Nu Bhoot.

name: Dev Joshi
bio: A young Gujarati actor based in Mumbai with experience across national-level one-act play competitions, experimental and commercial Gujarati theatre, short films, and advertisements. With Ideas Unlimited, he has performed in Mareez, Kaagdo, 3rd Bell, Tabiyat, and Lagan Ma Magan. He has won Best Actor at the National Level Gujarati Mono Act Competition and placed second at the National Level Gujarati One Act Play Competition. His upcoming Gujarati feature film Desai Vs Sardesai is currently in post-production.

name: Nilesh Joshi
bio: A Gujarati theatre actor who joined the cast of Ideas Unlimited's Apurva Avsar, replacing Pratik Gandhi in the role. He performs alongside Dharmendra Gohil and Pulkit Solanki in this three-actor dramatization of the life of Shrimad Rajchandra.

name: Subhash Ashar
bio: A set designer for Ideas Unlimited Productions. He designed the sets for Apurva Avsar, creating the staging for a play that spans centuries of Jain spiritual history.

name: Pritesh Sodha
bio: A sound and lighting designer who has worked with Ideas Unlimited Productions on Apurva Avsar and Apoorav Khela.

name: Dr. Dhanvant Shah
bio: A Jain scholar and writer who wrote the script for Ideas Unlimited's Apoorav Khela, dramatising the life of the 17th-century Jain mystical poet Avdhoot Anandghan from the scant oral histories and legends that survive.

name: Ashok Parmar
bio: A theatre actor who performed with Ideas Unlimited in Apoorav Khela, the biographical play on the Jain saint-poet Anandghan.

name: Nimesh Dave
bio: A theatre actor who performed with Ideas Unlimited in Apoorav Khela.

name: Manish Rohit
bio: A theatre actor who performed with Ideas Unlimited in Apoorav Khela.

name: Rajesh Mandloi
bio: A language consultant for Ideas Unlimited's Apoorav Khela, working on the Rajasthani dialect that gives the play its distinctive folk texture.

name: Keyuri Shah
bio: A Gujarati and Hindi theatre actress with 15 years of stage experience, Keyuri Shah has built a prolific career across theatre, film, and advertising. With Ideas Unlimited, she has performed in 3rd Bell, Lagan Ma Magan, and iSchool. Her screen work spans Hindi, Gujarati, Marathi, and Rajasthani cinema — including Rizwan, Kaash Tum Hote, Jaggu Ani Juliet, and over ten Gujarati feature films. She has also written and directed Ek Deewani Ek Mastani, a 90-minute Hindi/Gujarati monologue. She has worked in commercials for Godrej, Tata Nano, and Aeroplane Masala.
